title: Understanding Angular Velocity
text: Angular velocity is the rate of change of angular displacement and is usually measured in radians per second. To find the angular velocity of a tire, we need to relate the linear speed of the car to the rotational speed of the tire.

title: Convert Speed to Meters per Second
text: First, convert the speed of the automobile from kilometers per hour to meters per second. Remember that $$1$$ $$\frac{km}{h}$$ is equal to $$\frac{1}{3.6}$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$.

title: Relate Linear Speed to Angular Velocity
text: The linear speed (v) of the car is related to the angular velocity (ω) of the tire by the formula: $$v=r \omega$$, where $$r$$ is the radius of the tire.

title: Calculate the Radius of the Tire
text: The diameter of the tire is $$75.0$$ cm. What is the radius in meters?

title: Calculate Angular Velocity
text: Using the formula $$v=r \omega$$, calculate the angular velocity of the tire. Use $$v=25$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ and $$r=0.375$$ $$m$$.
